mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer income and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces positive returns in fewer than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate above 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates short-term discount rates and slows credit creation, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with month-over-month declines in industrial production signals that monetary tightening is transmitting into real output contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During housing contraction regimes, month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal weakening household balance-sheet effects and falling discretionary demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods of declining HOUST, falling RSAFS is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the conditional Sharpe ratio of the signal is not lower than the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding manufacturing output and operating leverage that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a turning point in the labor market that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger condition occurs but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate a negative real policy shock that erodes discount-rate confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months, or the interaction term does not improve prediction R-squared by at least 0.02 over the main effects alone.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal weakening personal consumption expenditures and forward earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return under the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
